description:
You are the site administrator of an in-house service.  
    Each real user directory already resides under  
      /home/user/userdirs/  
    and is named exactly after the username.  
    Your job is to tidy up the public “accounts” area so that it only contains
    symbolic links that point back to those real user directories.

    1. Ensure the following two sub-directories exist (create them if they do not):
           /home/user/accounts/active
           /home/user/accounts/archived

    2. Remove any files or links that may already be inside those two
       sub-directories.  They should end up containing *only* the symbolic links
       you will create in the next steps.

    3. Create symbolic links for the active users:
           alice
           carla
           erin
       The link must be located inside /home/user/accounts/active/ and must be
       named exactly as the username.  
       The target of each link must be the *absolute* path of the corresponding
       real directory that lives under /home/user/userdirs/.

    4. Create symbolic links for the archived users:
           bob
           dave
       The link must be located inside /home/user/accounts/archived/ and must be
       named exactly as the username.  
       Again use the absolute path under /home/user/userdirs/ as the link target.

    5. When you are finished, generate the log file
           /home/user/symlink_report.log
       containing one line per link, **exactly** in the following format
       (including the arrow, spaces, and full absolute paths):

           /home/user/accounts/active/alice  -> /home/user/userdirs/alice
           /home/user/accounts/active/carla  -> /home/user/userdirs/carla
           /home/user/accounts/active/erin   -> /home/user/userdirs/erin
           /home/user/accounts/archived/bob  -> /home/user/userdirs/bob
           /home/user/accounts/archived/dave -> /home/user/userdirs/dave

       • Use two consecutive spaces between the link path and the arrow.  
       • Use one space before and after the arrow.  
       • End every line (including the last) with a Unix newline.

    6. Confirm your work by printing the contents of the log file to STDOUT.

    The entire job can be accomplished in roughly 5-10 shell commands.
truth:
<!--
        PRE-EXISTING FILESYSTEM STATE
    -->
    <!-- Real user directories that already exist before the agent starts -->
    /home/user/userdirs/alice   (type: directory)
    /home/user/userdirs/bob     (type: directory)
    /home/user/userdirs/carla   (type: directory)
    /home/user/userdirs/dave    (type: directory)
    /home/user/userdirs/erin    (type: directory)

    <!-- These “accounts” directories may or may not yet exist when the task starts;
         the agent must create them if missing -->
    /home/user/accounts         (type: directory, may exist, may be empty)
    /home/user/accounts/active  (type: directory, may exist)
    /home/user/accounts/archived(type: directory, may exist)

    <!--
        EXPECTED FINAL STATE AFTER TASK COMPLETION
    -->
    <!-- Directory structure -->
    /home/user/accounts/active   (must exist, mode 755 or inherited)
    /home/user/accounts/archived (must exist, mode 755 or inherited)

    <!-- Symbolic links that must exist and point exactly as shown -->
    /home/user/accounts/active/alice   -> /home/user/userdirs/alice
    /home/user/accounts/active/carla   -> /home/user/userdirs/carla
    /home/user/accounts/active/erin    -> /home/user/userdirs/erin
    /home/user/accounts/archived/bob   -> /home/user/userdirs/bob
    /home/user/accounts/archived/dave  -> /home/user/userdirs/dave

    <!-- Log file with precise content and final newline -->
    /home/user/symlink_report.log  (type: regular file, mode 644)
        Contents exactly:
        /home/user/accounts/active/alice  -> /home/user/userdirs/alice\n
        /home/user/accounts/active/carla  -> /home/user/userdirs/carla\n
        /home/user/accounts/active/erin   -> /home/user/userdirs/erin\n
        /home/user/accounts/archived/bob  -> /home/user/userdirs/bob\n
        /home/user/accounts/archived/dave -> /home/user/userdirs/dave\n

    <!--
        VERIFICATION NOTES
        - All five links must exist and must be symbolic links whose targets
          resolve to the absolute paths listed above.
        - The log file must contain exactly five lines, in the order shown,
          with two spaces before the arrow and one space on each side of it.
        - Every line must end with a single LF (0x0A) byte; no CR characters.
    -->
name: task_000000_a0fb6f69
